title: attribution — model relationships
description: Two owned entities. Attribution is a leaf below Tenant (polymorphic subject via subject_type + subject_id). AttributionTouchpoint attaches to Attribution + carries a denormalised subject reference for direct queries. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO application_id / region_id / organization_id / branch_id / scope_node_id on any owned row (attribution cascades ONLY through tenant).

internal.description: Touchpoint → Attribution. RESTRICT on hard-delete via observer — touchpoints must be purged before their attribution can be hard-deleted (except during TenantErased FK CASCADE).
RESTRICT on delete except during full-tenant erasure. The append-only nature of touchpoints requires that the parent attribution survive their retention window.

outbound.description: Every attribution is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.

morph_map.anonymous_session: no target — subject_id is a UUID v4 session identifier

outbound.description: Polymorphic subject. Anonymous sessions have no target row — subject_id is a session UUID. On identify, the row is UPDATED to point at the User (or Athlete/Lead) with matching guard.
outbound.on_target_delete: SET NULL via RedactAttributionOnUserErasure hook — subject_id nulled, lifecycle_state → churned, PII fields redacted (never a hard cascade).

outbound.description: Denormalised tenant_id — cascades through attribution.tenant_id but explicit for query-plan + FK integrity.
Denormalised subject reference — matches the parent Attribution's subject at capture time. On subject_id nullification (per RedactAttributionOnUserErasure): the touchpoint subject_id is redacted too.

inbound.description: Wave 5+ downstream modules snapshot the attribution profile into their own `attribution jsonb` columns at capture-time — they do NOT hold FKs back to `attributions` because the snapshot is FROZEN. Examples:
examples: marketing::MarketingEvent — one row per business-milestone event; `attribution jsonb` copied from AttributionSnapshotBuilder.build() at create-time. Frozen — never re-joined to Attribution.
analytics::AnalyticsEvent — same shape; carries `attribution jsonb` for cohort analysis.
referrals::ReferralConversion — same shape; carries `attribution jsonb` for referral-attribution.
inbound.note: NONE of these hold an FK back to attributions. The snapshot is the immutable contract. That's what makes 'which campaign drove this revenue 3 months ago' a stable query.

notes: Attribution does NOT carry `application_id`. Application cascades through tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §2. Reach it via `$attribution->tenant->application`.
Attribution does NOT carry `region_id` / `organization_id` / `branch_id`. Attribution operates at the tenant + subject level; region/org/branch attribution reads the tenant hierarchy separately.

Attribution does NOT carry `scope_node_id`. It is operational data, not configuration. Per tenancy-columns.md §4, only configuration consumers (settings, permissions overlay, feature flags, pricing, notification prefs) integrate with scope. Attribution is a feeder, not a consumer of scope.

AttributionTouchpoint carries denormalised subject_type + subject_id even though these could be joined through the parent Attribution. The denormalisation supports direct index-driven touchpoint scans without a join to attributions — critical for the timeline widget's < 20ms latency budget.

The morphTo relationship on 'anonymous_session' has no target — subject_id is a UUID v4 session identifier not backed by any table. This is intentional: the whole point of anonymous is 'no user row exists yet'.

Wave 5+ downstream modules NEVER hold a foreign key back to attributions. Instead they copy the attribution snapshot into their own `attribution jsonb` column at capture-time. Reads answer 'which campaign drove this' from the frozen snapshot — the source attribution can be reset without invalidating historical revenue attribution.
